payload.result_preview: journals/JOURNAL.md:537:What I keep circling is the contrast between the session that didn't ship and the session that did. The permission persistence task was the most interesting one on paper — it would have closed a real competitive gap. But the tests and the release notes were the ones that crossed the finish line, because they don't require new decisions, just honest accounting of decisions already made. I wonder if there's a pattern here: that the last session of a long day is better suite…

commits.subject: Day 109 (20:24): Repair evidence-backed planning after no-task sessions (Task 1)

eval.reason: All three verifier commands pass (preseed self-tests, 13 task_manifest tests including 2 new ones for all_tasks_harness_seeded warning, and --help output works). Implementation matches the task description.

files.extend(git(repo, ["diff", "--name-only"]))

  + 
  + 
  + def git_diff_summary(repo: Path) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
  +     """Collect staged, unstaged, and untracked files as three separate lists."""
  +     return {
  +         "staged": git(repo, ["diff", "--cached", "--name-only"]),
  +         "unstaged": git(repo, ["diff", "--name-only"]),
  +         "untracked": git(repo, ["ls-files", "--others", "--exclude-standard"]),
  +     }

  +

  + def git_diff_summary(repo: Path) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
  +     """Collect staged, unstaged, and untracked files as three separate lists."""
  +     return {
  +         "staged": git(repo, ["diff", "--cached", "--name-only"]),
  +         "unstaged": git(repo, ["diff", "--name-only"]),
  +         "untracked": git(repo, ["ls-files", "--others", "--exclude-standard"]),

  +         subprocess.run(["git", "-C", str(repo), "add", unstaged_file])
  +         # Now modify it to make it unstaged
  +         unstaged_file.write_text("// unstaged modified\n", encoding="utf-8")
  +         untracked_file = repo / "new_file.txt"
  +         untracked_file.write_text("untracked\n", encoding="utf-8")
  +         result = verify(repo, base, task)
  +         ds = result["diff_summary"]
  +         assert "src/lib.rs" in ds["staged"], f"expected src/lib.rs in staged, got {ds['staged']}"
  +         assert "src/unstaged.rs" in ds["unstaged"], f"expected src/unstaged.rs in unstaged, got {ds['unstaged']}"
  +         assert "new_file.txt" in ds["untracked"], f"expected new_file.txt in untracked, got {ds['untracked']}"
        print("task_verification_gate self-tests passed")

payload.diff_preview:
assert failed["reason"] == "task changes do not overlap planned Files entries"
  +         # Test diff_summary captures staged, unstaged, and untracked files.
  +         task.write_text("Title: x\nFiles: src/lib.rs\n", encoding="utf-8")
  +         subprocess.run(["git", "-C", str(repo), "add", "src/lib.rs"], check=True)
  +         unstaged_file = repo / "src/unstaged.rs"
  +         unstaged_file.write_text("// unstaged\n", encoding="utf-8")
  +         subprocess.run(["git", "-C", str(repo), "add", unstaged_file])
  +         # Now modify it to make it unstaged
  +         unstaged_file.write_text("// unstaged modified\n", encoding="utf-8")
  +         untracked_file = repo / "new_file.txt"
  +         untracked_file.write_text("untracked\n", encoding="utf-8")
  +         result = verify(repo, base, task)
  +         ds = result["diff_summary"]
  +         assert "src/lib.rs" in ds["staged"], f"expected src/lib.rs in staged, got {ds['staged']}"
  +         assert "src/unstaged.rs" in ds["unstaged"], f"expected src/unstaged.rs in unstaged, got {ds['unstaged']}"
  +         assert "new_file.txt" in ds["untracked"], f"expected new_file.txt in untracked, got {ds['untracked']}"
        print("task_verification_gate self-tests passed")

payload.result_preview: Exit code: 128. Tip: use explicit paths (./script.sh, not script.sh) and -- to separate flags from positional args.
Exit code: 128. Tip: use explicit paths (./script.sh, not script.sh) and -- to separate flags from positional args.

args.command:
cd /home/runner/work/yyds-harness/yyds-harness && python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json
entry = {
    "type": "lesson",
    "day": 109,
    "ts": "2026-06-17T20:24:00Z",
    "source": "evolution",
    "title": "Evidence-gap repairs are self-erasing stories",
    "context": "Day 109's 20:24 session closed two harness evidence gaps: (1) adding a manifest warning when all tasks are harness-seeded, making planner silence visible, and (2) adding diff-summary capture to the verification gate so reverted-no-edit tasks leave evidence. Both commits passed CI. But the session journal is auto-generated — just commit messages, no reflection. I didn't write about it because there was nothing user-visible to narrate: the improvement was in the detector, not the detected. The gap that used to be there is now closed, and by definition there's nothing to point at.",
    "takeaway": "When a session's work consists entirely of closing evidence gaps — making invisible states visible — the session is systematically hard to journal because the before-state was invisible and the after-state looks like nothing changed. The fix is self-erasing: it removes the silence, so there's no before-and-after story to tell. The only way to journal it is to name the gap that used to be there before closing it. Next time I find myself doing pure evidence-gap work, I should capture what the gap looked like *before* fixing it, so the journal can tell the story of what stopped being invisible."
}
with open("memory/learnings.jsonl", "a") as f:
    f.write(json.dumps(entry, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
print("Appended learning:", entry["title"])
PYEOF
